title: Urgent Walk-In Clinic
description: A mental health and addiction crisis walk-in clinic open seven days a week, serving anyone in Multnomah County regardless of insurance, income, or age. Care may include counseling, psychiatric prescriber services, and referrals. Sliding-scale fees for the uninsured.
engage: Walk in at 4212 SE Division St, Suite 100, Portland, or call 503-963-2575. Open 7 days a week — call to confirm current hours.
content:
Services

Immediate mental health and addiction crisis support based on your clinical needs, which may include:

  • Counseling and crisis stabilization
  • Psychiatric prescriber services
  • Referrals to affordable ongoing mental health care
  • Substance use treatment referrals
  • Connections to other community resources

Who It's For

Anyone in Multnomah County experiencing a mental health or addiction crisis — regardless of insurance, income, or age. Sliding-scale fees are available for people who are uninsured.

Location & Hours

4212 SE Division St, Suite 100, Portland. Open seven days a week; call 503-963-2575 to confirm hours.

title: Community & Clinic Based Services
description: Outpatient, evidence-based treatment for people of all ages with mental health, addiction, and dual-diagnosis needs — assessment and treatment planning, individual and group counseling, case management, skills training, supported employment and education, and medication management.
engage: Call the Cascadia Access, Information & Referral line at 503-674-7777 (Mon–Fri, 9am–5pm) to get connected.
content:
Services

  • Comprehensive assessment and treatment planning
  • Individual and group counseling
  • Case management
  • Skills training
  • Supported employment and supported education
  • Medication management
  • Consultation

Who It's For

People of all ages living with mental health, addiction, or co-occurring (dual-diagnosis) conditions.

How to Access

Call the Cascadia Access, Information & Referral line at 503-674-7777, Monday–Friday, 9am–5pm.

title: Project Respond (Mobile Crisis Team)
description: A 24/7 mobile crisis response team of master's-level mental health clinicians that responds in the community to non-violent mental health and welfare crises. Dispatched through Multnomah County's crisis line.
engage: Reach Project Respond through the Multnomah County Crisis Line at 503-988-4888 (24/7).
content:
What It Is

Project Respond is a 24/7 mobile crisis response team that has operated for close to 20 years. Teams — each made up of two master's-level mental health clinicians — respond in the community to non-violent calls involving mental health or welfare concerns.

How to Access

Project Respond is dispatched through Multnomah County's Crisis Line at 503-988-4888, available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

Who It's For

Anyone in Multnomah County experiencing a mental health crisis.

title: Garlington Health Center
description: An integrated health center offering coordinated primary care and behavioral healthcare for people living with mental illness or facing addiction — including outpatient mental health and substance use services and psychiatric rehabilitation. Co-located with affordable housing.
engage: Call reception at 503-283-3763 or the intake line at 503-674-7777 (Mon–Fri 9am–5pm). Located at 3036 NE MLK Jr Blvd, Portland.
content:
Services

  • Coordinated primary care and behavioral healthcare
  • Outpatient mental health and substance use services
  • Psychiatric rehabilitation services
  • Crisis mental health services, including access to 24-hour mobile crisis teams

Who It's For

People diagnosed with mental illness or facing addiction challenges. The Garlington campus also includes affordable apartments, with units reserved for veterans and people experiencing serious mental illness.

Location & Hours

3036 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Portland, OR 97212. Reception 503-283-3763; phone intake Mon–Fri 9am–5pm.
street_address: 3036 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd

title: Immigration Legal Services
description: A nonprofit law program offering low-cost consultations and legal representation to immigrants and refugees across Oregon and southwest Washington — family petitions, green cards, citizenship, DACA, VAWA and U/T-visas for survivors, TPS, asylum, and removal defense.
engage: Call 503-542-2855 or email ils-intakes@ccoregon.org to request a consultation. By appointment only — walk-ins are not accepted.
content:
Legal Services Offered

  • Family-based applications and visas
  • Permanent residency (Green Card)
  • Naturalization and citizenship
  • Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)
  • VAWA protections for domestic violence and sexual assault survivors
  • U-Visas for crime victims
  • T-Visas for trafficking survivors
  • Temporary Protected Status (TPS)
  • Asylum and removal defense
  • Refugee adjustment and family petitions

Who They Serve

Families separated by immigration, refugees, DACA recipients, crime and trafficking survivors, and low-income individuals and families seeking documentation, throughout Oregon and southwest Washington.

How to Access

Call 503-542-2855 or email ils-intakes@ccoregon.org. Low-cost consultations. By appointment only — walk-ins will not be accepted.
centerings: Immigrants, Refugees, Asylum Seekers; Survivors of Human Trafficking; Domestic Violence (DV) Survivors

title: Minutes That Matter
description: Free prepaid international calling cards for deployed U.S. service members, so troops can call home at no cost. The program has provided over 400 million minutes of airtime.
engage: Request calling cards through the online form at cellphonesforsoldiers.com, or call 800-982-0972.
content:
What It Provides

Free prepaid international calling cards that let deployed service members call home completely free of charge.

How to Request

Fill out the online request form with the requestor's name, recipient name, address, and number of cards needed. Once verified, the calling cards are mailed to the address provided.

Who It's For

Deployed U.S. service members and their families.

title: Helping Heroes Home
description: One-time emergency financial assistance for veterans facing reintegration challenges — help with a cell phone and service, a computer and internet, rent to avoid eviction, essential car repairs, and more.
engage: Apply online at cellphonesforsoldiers.com or call 800-982-0972.
content:
What It Provides

One-time emergency financial assistance to veterans for needs such as:

  • A cell phone and service
  • A computer and internet
  • Rent, to avoid eviction
  • Imperative car repairs
  • Other emergency reintegration needs

Who It's For

U.S. veterans facing communication barriers or emergency reintegration challenges.

How to Apply

Apply online at cellphonesforsoldiers.com.

title: Free Phone for Veterans (Mobile)
description: A free smartphone with one year of unlimited talk, text, and data for low-income and homeless veterans. Requires proof of military service (DD-214) and a short application.
content:
What It Provides

A free smartphone plus one year of unlimited talk, text, and data.

Eligibility

  • Veteran of the U.S. Armed Forces
  • Demonstrated financial need (prioritizing low-income and at-risk veterans)
  • Proof of military service (typically a copy of your DD-214)
  • Completed short application form

How to Apply

Visit the Veteran Phone Application page at cellphonesforsoldiers.com/veteran-mobile-program-request, or call 800-982-0972.
